Pooled 3-Cohort Neuropsych: Class 1 Life-Course Plots

Minghui (Grace) Wang

2026-07-09

0. Overview

This script generates Class 1 life-course plots for the pooled 3-cohort neuropsychological models. It loads fitted model objects from RDS (saved by 01_fit_models.Rmd) instead of refitting.

Run neuropsych_pooled3cohorts_03_plot_lifecourse_curves_model2_crude.Rmd (notebook 03) first. This script reads the y-axis range table saved by the Class 2 script so that both sets of plots share the same y-axis scale per test.

The Class 1 model uses age at measurement as the time scale. For each test, the figure overlays model-predicted cognitive score curves on a background scatter plot showing empirical data support.

0.1 Choose analysis sample

SAMPLE <- "2visit"
covariates <- NULL
source("R/setup_analysis_sample.R")
model_tag <- if (SAMPLE == "2visit") "crude_2visit" else "crude"
dir_tag   <- if (SAMPLE == "2visit") "_2visit" else ""

plot_dir <- paste0(
  "../output/plots/pooled3_04_lifecourse_plots_class1_crude", dir_tag
)

dir.create(plot_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

1. Load fitted Class 1 model objects

model_rds_dir <- "../output/models"

class1_fits <- readRDS(
  file.path(
    model_rds_dir,
    "pooled3_crude_2visit_class1_fits.rds"
  )
)
class1_fits_success <- class1_fits |>
  filter(fit_success)

cat(
  "Loaded", nrow(class1_fits_success),
  "successfully fitted Class 1 model strata\n"
)
## Loaded 28 successfully fitted Class 1 model strata
class1_fits_success |>
  select(
    test,
    sex_label,
    n_obs,
    n_subjects,
    subject_random_slope_used
  ) |>
  knitr::kable()
test sex_label n_obs n_subjects subject_random_slope_used
coding_correct Women 1800 874 TRUE
coding_correct Men 1599 775 TRUE
dsb Women 5683 2003 TRUE
dsb Men 4186 1553 TRUE
dsf Women 5789 2037 TRUE
dsf Men 4260 1573 TRUE
lmd Women 7244 2313 TRUE
lmd Men 5537 1863 TRUE
lmi Women 7298 2319 TRUE
lmi Men 5570 1870 TRUE
lmr Women 6164 1975 TRUE
lmr Men 4715 1580 TRUE
pasd Women 6009 1936 TRUE
pasd Men 4612 1549 TRUE
pasi Women 6715 2115 TRUE
pasi Men 5063 1678 TRUE
pasr Women 3419 1428 TRUE
pasr Men 2663 1128 TRUE
trailsa Women 6109 2027 TRUE
trailsa Men 4925 1698 TRUE
trailsb Women 5861 1968 TRUE
trailsb Men 4769 1662 TRUE
vrd Women 6988 2266 TRUE
vrd Men 5471 1848 TRUE
vri Women 7038 2275 TRUE
vri Men 5514 1858 TRUE

vrr Men 5150 1708 TRUE

2. Prepare test dictionary and Class 1 prediction grid

test_dictionary_sheet <- make_pooled_test_dictionary( #  defined in R/workbook_writers.R
  model_ready_pool 
)

class1_plot_ages <- seq(
  30,
  90,
  by = 1
)

test_dictionary_sheet |>
  knitr::kable()
test test_label xprize_subdomain range
dsf Digit Span Forward Attention 0-9
dsb Digit Span Backward Executive Function & Processing 0-8
trailsb Trail Making Test Part B Executive Function & Processing 0.32-10.00 minutes; maximum time set at 10 minutes
lmd Logical Memory, delayed recall Memory 0-23
lmi Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmr Logical Memory, recognition Memory 0-11
pasd Paired Associates, delayed recall Memory 0-10
pasi Paired Associates, immediate recall Memory 0-21
pasr Paired Associates, recognition Memory 0-10
coding_correct WAIS-IV Coding, total correct Psychomotor 0-119
trailsa Trail Making Test Part A Psychomotor 0.32-10.00 minutes; maximum time set at 10 minutes
vrd Visual Reproductions, delayed recall Visual Learning 0-14
vri Visual Reproductions, immediate recall Visual Learning 0-14
vrr Visual Reproductions, recognition Visual Learning 0-14

3. Create Class 1 prediction curves

make_class1_prediction_curve <- function(model_x, data_x) {
  
  emm_x <- emmeans::emmeans(
    model_x,
    specs = ~ cage_65,
    at = list(
      cage_65 = class1_plot_ages - 65,
      birth_year_c = 0
    ),
    data = data_x,
    lmer.df = "asymptotic"
  )
  
  summary(emm_x) |>
    as_tibble() |>
    clean_names() |>
    mutate(
      age = cage_65 + 65
    ) |>
    select(
      age,
      predicted_score = emmean,
      se,
      everything()
    )
}
class1_prediction_curves <- class1_fits_success |>
  mutate(
    prediction_curve = map2(
      model,
      data,
      ~ make_class1_prediction_curve(
        model_x = .x,
        data_x = .y
      )
    )
  ) |>
  select(
    test,
    sex_label,
    prediction_curve
  ) |>
  unnest(
    prediction_curve
  ) |>
  left_join(
    test_dictionary_sheet |>
      select(
        test,
        test_label,
        xprize_subdomain,
        range
      ),
    by = "test"
  ) |>
  arrange(
    xprize_subdomain,
    test,
    sex_label,
    age
  )

class1_prediction_curves |>
  filter(
    test == "lmi"
  ) |>
  head(20) |>
  knitr::kable(digits = 2)
test sex_label age predicted_score se cage_65 df asymp_lcl asymp_ucl test_label xprize_subdomain range
lmi Women 30 8.24 1.00 -35 Inf 6.29 10.19 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmi Women 31 8.53 0.95 -34 Inf 6.67 10.40 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmi Women 32 8.81 0.91 -33 Inf 7.03 10.60 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmi Women 33 9.09 0.87 -32 Inf 7.39 10.79 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmi Women 34 9.35 0.83 -31 Inf 7.73 10.96 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmi Women 35 9.60 0.78 -30 Inf 8.06 11.13 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmi Women 36 9.84 0.74 -29 Inf 8.38 11.30 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmi Women 37 10.07 0.70 -28 Inf 8.69 11.45 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmi Women 38 10.29 0.66 -27 Inf 9.00 11.59 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmi Women 39 10.50 0.62 -26 Inf 9.28 11.72 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmi Women 40 10.70 0.58 -25 Inf 9.56 11.84 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmi Women 41 10.89 0.54 -24 Inf 9.83 11.96 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmi Women 42 11.08 0.50 -23 Inf 10.09 12.06 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmi Women 43 11.25 0.46 -22 Inf 10.34 12.16 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmi Women 44 11.41 0.43 -21 Inf 10.57 12.24 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmi Women 45 11.56 0.39 -20 Inf 10.80 12.32 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmi Women 46 11.70 0.35 -19 Inf 11.01 12.39 Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23

4. Class 1 · Age as Time life-course figures

  • The background layer shows observed cognitive scores by age at measurement, with cohort indicated by point shape and sex indicated by color.
  • The foreground layer shows the fitted Class 1 model fixed-effect curves for women and men.

4.1 Plot settings

plot_age_min <- 30
plot_age_max <- 90

sex_colors <- c(
  "Women" = "#CC4C8A",
  "Men" = "#0E63AC"
)

cohort_shapes <- c(
  "Original" = 17,
  "Offspring" = 16,
  "Gen3" = 15
)

background_point_alpha <- 0.18
background_point_size <- 0.85

curve_linewidth <- 1.4
# Load shared y-axis settings from codebook 03.

class2_y_axis_table <- readRDS(
  paste0(
    "../output/tables/class2_y_axis_table_for_lifecourse_plots", dir_tag, ".rds"
  )
)

class2_y_axis_table |>
  knitr::kable(digits = 2)
test y_min y_max y_breaks
coding_correct 20.0 120.0 20, 40, 60, 80, 100, 120
dsb 4.0 6.0 4.0, 4.5, 5.0, 5.5, 6.0
dsf 5.5 9.0 5.5, 6.0, 6.5, 7.0, 7.5, 8.0, 8.5, 9.0
lmd 4.0 16.0 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16
lmi 6.0 16.0 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16
lmr 7.0 13.0 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13
pasd 4.0 14.0 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14
pasi 8.0 22.0 8, 10, 12, 14, 16, 18, 20, 22
pasr 4.0 14.0 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14
trailsa -0.5 2.5 -0.5, 0.0, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 2.5
trailsb -4.0 10.0 -4, -2, 0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10
vrd 0.0 14.0 0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14
vri 2.0 14.0 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14
vrr 1.5 5.0 1.5, 2.0, 2.5, 3.0, 3.5, 4.0, 4.5, 5.0 
missing_axis_tests <- class1_prediction_curves |>
  distinct(test) |>
  anti_join(
    class2_y_axis_table |>
      distinct(test),
    by = "test"
  )

if (nrow(missing_axis_tests) > 0) {
  stop(
    "Missing Class 2 y-axis range for: ",
    paste(missing_axis_tests$test, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

4.2 Define plotting function

plot_class1_age_support_one_test <- function(
    scatter_data,
    curve_data,
    test_dictionary,
    test_x,
    y_axis_table = NULL
) {
  
  scatter_x <- scatter_data |>
    filter(
      test == test_x,
      complete_for_model,
      age_at_measure >= plot_age_min,
      age_at_measure <= plot_age_max
    ) |>
    drop_na(
      age_at_measure,
      score,
      sex_label,
      idtype_label
    ) |>
    mutate(
      sex_label = factor(
        sex_label,
        levels = c("Women", "Men")
      ),
      idtype_label = factor(
        idtype_label,
        levels = c("Original", "Offspring", "Gen3")
      )
    )
  
  curve_x <- curve_data |>
    filter(
      test == test_x,
      age >= plot_age_min,
      age <= plot_age_max
    ) |>
    drop_na(
      age,
      predicted_score,
      sex_label
    ) |>
    mutate(
      sex_label = factor(
        sex_label,
        levels = c("Women", "Men")
      )
    )
  
  test_label_x <- test_dictionary |>
    filter(test == test_x) |>
    distinct(test_label) |>
    pull(test_label) |>
    first()

  
  if (is.null(y_axis_table)) {
    
    y_axis_x <- make_curve_y_axis(
      curve_x = curve_x,
      y_var = "predicted_score"
    )
    
    y_limits_x <- y_axis_x$limits
    y_breaks_x <- y_axis_x$breaks
    
  } else {
    
    y_axis_x <- y_axis_table |>
      filter(test == test_x) |>
      slice(1)
    
    y_limits_x <- c(
      y_axis_x$y_min,
      y_axis_x$y_max
    )
    
    y_breaks_x <- y_axis_x$y_breaks[[1]]
  }
  
  ggplot() +
    geom_point(
      data = scatter_x,
      aes(
        x = age_at_measure,
        y = score,
        color = sex_label,
        shape = idtype_label
      ),
      alpha = background_point_alpha,
      size = background_point_size
    ) +
    geom_ribbon(
      data = curve_x,
      aes(
        x = age,
        ymin = asymp_lcl,
        ymax = asymp_ucl,
        fill = sex_label
      ),
      alpha = 0.12,
      color = NA
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = curve_x,
      aes(
        x = age,
        y = predicted_score,
        color = sex_label
      ),
      linewidth = curve_linewidth
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = sex_colors,
      name = "Sex"
    ) +
    scale_shape_manual(
      values = cohort_shapes,
      name = "Cohort"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      limits = c(plot_age_min, plot_age_max),
      breaks = seq(plot_age_min, plot_age_max, by = 10)
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      name = "Cognitive score",
      breaks = y_breaks_x
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(
      ylim = y_limits_x
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(
        "Class 1 · Age as Time: ",
        test_label_x
      ),
      subtitle = "Observed scores in background with fitted age-as-time curves by sex",
      x = "Age at measurement"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11.5, color = "grey30"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 13),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 10.5),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
}
plot_class1_age_support_one_test(
  scatter_data = model_ready_analysis,
  curve_data = class1_prediction_curves,
  test_dictionary = test_dictionary_sheet,
  test_x = "lmd",
  y_axis_table = class2_y_axis_table
)

4.3 Save all Class 1 age-support figures

class1_plot_test_order <- class1_prediction_curves |>
  distinct(
    xprize_subdomain,
    test
  ) |>
  arrange(
    xprize_subdomain,
    test
  )

walk2(
  class1_plot_test_order$xprize_subdomain,
  class1_plot_test_order$test,
  \(subdomain_x, test_x) {
    
    plot_x <- plot_class1_age_support_one_test(
      scatter_data = model_ready_analysis,
      curve_data = class1_prediction_curves,
      test_dictionary = test_dictionary_sheet,
      test_x = test_x,
      y_axis_table = class2_y_axis_table
    )
    
    subdomain_file_x <- subdomain_x |>
      str_to_lower() |>
      str_replace_all("&", "and") |>
      str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9]+", "_") |>
      str_replace_all("_+", "_") |>
      str_replace_all("^_|_$", "")
    
    test_file_x <- test_x |>
      str_to_lower() |>
      str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9]+", "_") |>
      str_replace_all("_+", "_") |>
      str_replace_all("^_|_$", "")
    
    ggsave(
      filename = file.path(
        plot_dir,
        paste0(
          "pooled3_class1_age_as_time_lifecourse_curve_",
          subdomain_file_x,
          "_",
          test_file_x,
          "_",
          output_suffix,
          ".pdf"
        )
      ),
      plot = plot_x,
      width = 11,
      height = 6
    )
  }
)
